rulings.Case Title: MILA POPOV VS. G&P CONSTRUCTION INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Fine By Design To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT FINE BY DESIGN INC., Motion By Defendant Fine By Design To Enforce Settlement. Granted. Pursuant to CCP sec. 664.6, the parties entered into a binding, enforceable settlement. The August 14, 2014 hearing transcript establishes that Defendant agreed to pay Plaintiff $10,000 in exchange for a release of all claims. (Declaration of Peter Linn, Ex. A., Hearing Transcript, pp. 7:6-8:1.) Both parties stated on the record that they understood the terms of the settlement and agreed to them. (Id. at pp. 8:11-9:12.) Judge Sing concluded that the matter had been "resolved and settled." (Id. at p. 9: 13-14.) Thus, the terms agreed to on the record are binding and enforceable. Defendant's request for attorney's fees is denied. The parties did not agree to an attorney's fee provision on the record. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA WONG VS. NEURALTUS PHARMACEUTICALS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANT NEURALTUS PHARMACEUTICALS INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. As to the cause of action for strict liability for design or manufacturing defect, the motion is granted. Defendant met its initial burden by presenting evidence that Plaintiff cannot provide evidence of a defect in the packaging. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts 12-16, 31.) Plaintiff does not raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether there was a defect in the packaging. The doctrine of res ipso loquitur cannot be used to prove a defect for strict liability claims. (McCurter v. Norton Co. (1968) 263 Cal.App.2d 402, 408.) As to the cause of action for negligence (including negligent design/manufacture), the motion is denied. Plaintiff submits sufficient evidence to support a res ipso loquitur theory against Defendant Sharp Corp. (Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts ("AMF") 6, 8-12.) Plaintiff raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant Sharp was Defendant Neuraltus's agent and therefore whether Defendant Neuraltus can be found liable on a respondeat superior theory. (AMF 3-4; See Garlock Sealing Technologies LLC v. NAK Sealing Technologies Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 937, 965, "[t]he existence of an agency relationship is a factual question for the trier of fact¿") A jury should decide whether Plaintiff can establish negligence based on these theories. Additionally, Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that an evidentiary doctrine must be pled in the complaint in order for it to be raised on summary judgment. As to the cause of action for negligent failure to warn, summary adjudication is granted. It is undisputed that Plaintiff did not read the warning sheet that was in the box. (UMF 10.) No legal causation exists between inadequate warnings and plaintiff's injuries where plaintiff failed to read defendant's warnings. (Ramirez v. Plough, Inc. (1993) 6 Cal.4th 539, 555-56.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA WONG VS. NEURALTUS PHARMACEUTICALS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Deft. Sharp Corporation Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, Line 9, Defendant Sharp Corporation Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Motion For Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. As to the cause of action for strict liability for design or manufacturing defect, the motion is granted. Defendant met its initial burden by presenting evidence that Plaintiff cannot provide evidence of a defect in the packaging. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts 11, 29-30, 33-34, 37-38, 40, 44, 48-64.) Plaintiff does not raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether there was a defect in the packaging. The doctrine of res ipso loquitur cannot be used to prove a defect for strict liability claims. (McCurter v. Norton Co. (1968) 263 Cal.App.2d 402, 408.) As to the cause of action for negligence, the motion is denied. Plaintiff submits sufficient evidence to support a res ipso loquitur theory. (Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts ("AMF") 7, 9-13, 17.) A jury should decide whether Plaintiff can establish negligence based on this theory. As to the cause of action for negligent failure to warn, summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether it was foreseeable that the package presented a substantial danger, given that a Medical Safety Data Sheet was in the box with warnings that exposure to the chemical in the drug could cause severe irritation and/or burns. (AMF 14.) A reasonable jury could determine that placing the warnings inside the box, instead of on the outside, amounts to negligence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RAOUL D KENNEDY AND MARTHA NOLTE KENNEDY, VS. FONTANA WEST APARTMENT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, Line 4, CROSS COMPLAINANT FONTANA WEST APARTMENT CORPORATION Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint. Granted. Cross-complainant shall file its proposed Second Amended Cross-Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. It is the established policy of the state to liberally permit amendments to pleadings at any stage of the proceedings. (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) Further, "if the motion to amend is timely made and the granting of the motion will not prejudice the opposing party, it is error to refuse permission to amend." (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) Cross-complainant is seeking leave to amend approximately three months after filing the original cross-complaint. Discovery in the case is in the preliminary stages. No trial date is set. Thus, the motion to amend is timely made and Plaintiffs cannot establish prejudice. Plaintiffs' request for bifurcation is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN (BATROA) FOR THE et al VS. ROBERT CROSS AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 11, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANTs ROBERT CROSS and SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP's MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant SIDEMAN & BANCROFT's unopposed motions are granted. Plaintiffs BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN and MICHAEL LEVINE's objections are overruled, but for those as to privileges and privacy. Plaintiffs BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN and MICHAEL LEVINE shall give verified and otherwise fully code-compliant further responses, and produce documents, without further objections, to all special interrogatories and requests for production enumerated in the motion within ten calendar days of service of the order, except as to the remaining objections. Any response or production as to which privilege and/or privacy objections are asserted must be specifically identified in a privilege/privacy log with reference to the specific interrogatory or request under which it falls. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $6,585.00 are awarded Defendant SIDEMAN & BANCROFT, LLP against Dimitriou & Associates, PC, and are payable within ten calendar days of the service of the order. Moving party must prepare a detailed written order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to hydelaw@pacbell.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA A BLAKE VS. HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT INC A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT PAMELA YOUNG, ROBERT GEBRON, JOSEPH COSTELLO, JOSEPH COSTELLO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh and eighth causes of action against all moving Defendants. No opposition was raised and for good cause shown. The Demurrer is overruled as to the alter ego allegations against Defendants Costello III and Costello Jr. as the FAC meets the fact pleading standard required by the Code of Civil Procedure. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the sixteenth cause of action for all moving Defendants. First, the discovery rule is inapplicable here because Plaintiff necessarily knew of the facts that rendered the alleged representation untrue throughout the course of her employment, and when Plaintiff discovered the legal ramifications of those facts is irrelevant. (CCP § 338(d).) However, the FAC also alleged that the misrepresentations continued throughout her employment. But, because this is a cause of action for fraud, the misrepresentations must be pled with sufficient particularity, and Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege with particularity any misrepresentations that fall within the statute of limitations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA A BLAKE VS. HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT INC A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 11, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer overruled as to the ninth through fifteenth causes of action. A required element of collateral estoppel is that the party against whom it is asserted was a party to the prior proceeding or in privity with a party to the prior proceeding. (Zevnik v. Superior Court (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 76, 82, 70 Cal.Rptr.3d 817.) Plaintiff clearly was not a party to the Labor Commission action. Further, Defendant's contention that Plaintiff was in privity with Palmer in that action is based on an incorrect application of the "virtual representative" concept articulated in Alvarez v. May Dept. Stores Co.(2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1223. In that case, the plaintiffs in the prior proceeding sought and intended to be class representatives, and the court found that plaintiffs in a subsequent action, who were not parties to the initial action, were barred from asserting the same class claims. Notably, the court stated that collateral estoppel did not lead to an unfair result because the subsequent plaintiffs remained "free to litigate the merits of their personal claims. (Id. at 1238.) Thus, where class certification was denied, non-party plaintiffs were precluded from bringing another class action, but were not precluded from pursing their individual claims. The procedural posture here is distinguishable because the prior proceeding was not a class action, and Palmer did not act as or intend to represent a class. As such, the theory of "virtual representative" is inapplicable to these facts because it cannot be said that this plaintiff has had a full and fair opportunity to litigate the issue. Moreover, the FAC is not irreconcilable, as Defendant contends, because it is not so specific as to the class allegations that the determination of the purported class can necessarily be said to include Palmer. The Demurrer is overruled as to the tenth and thirteenth causes of action because Plaintiff's correction does not amount to a material inconsistency, but rather a correction to a typographical error. The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the sixteenth cause of action. First, the discovery rule is inapplicable here because Plaintiff necessarily knew of the facts that rendered the alleged representation untrue throughout the course of her employment, and when Plaintiff discovered the legal ramifications of those facts is irrelevant. (CCP § 338(d).) However, the FAC also alleged that the misrepresentations continued throughout her employment. But, because this is a cause of action for fraud, the misrepresentations must be pled with sufficient particularity, and Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege with particularity any misrepresentations that fall within the statute of limitations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

Plaintiff failed to file a substantive opposition. The court transfers this action to the limited jurisdiction court per agreement of the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
